articles.title: Microsoft's Most Important Build 2026 Announcement Wasn't About AI

articles.title: Huawei nova Y74 goes official with a 6,620mAh battery
articles.description: Huawei appears to have silently unveiled the nova Y74 in global markets, roughly a year after launching the nova Y73. The budget 4G phone features a 6.67-inch display and a 6,620mAh battery, and appears to be a rebadged version of the Enjoy 90 Plus that debuted in China in March. The Huawei nova Y74 features a 6.67-inch LCD display with a 90Hz refresh rate, HD+ resolution, and up to 850 nits of peak brightness. Huawei has not disclosed the chipset powering the device, but given its striking resemblance to the Enjoy 90 Plus, it is likely using a Kirin 800-series processor. The...

articles.title: Fake Call Alert: Google Launches New Safeguard for Android Users
articles.description: Scammers are using voice cloning and call spoofing to cheat people. To stop this, Google has launched a new 'Fake Call Detection' feature. It will be available on Android 12 and newer phones. The feature checks if a call is genuine before you even answer and warns you if it's fake.

articles.description: Tamil Nadu projects: L&T will invest Rs 18,600 crore across a data centre expansion, electronics components manufacturing unit and offshore wind infrastructure project. The investments are set to generate 8,200 jobs and boost industrial growth.

articles.title: UK MP sues Musk's xAI over fake sexualised images
articles.description: A British lawmaker has filed a case at London's High Court against Elon Musk's xAI after its Grok chatbot tool was used to create fake sexualised images of her, including in a bikini. Jess Asato, of the ruling Labour Party, said she lodged the "High Court claim against xAI, the company behind Grok" on Wednesday.

articles.title: India to use artificial intelligence for machine-readable standards, says Consumer Affairs Secretary Khare
articles.description: India's Consumer Affairs Ministry is set to develop advanced, machine-readable standards using AI. This initiative aims to simplify compliance for industries. The Bureau of Indian Standards is transforming to support this modernization. The government is encouraging private sector testing infrastructure. Govt sets up 6 sector-specific groups to identify products to boost manufacturing, cut imports

articles.title: India-made Kawasaki W175 launched in USA: Price, changes
articles.description: Powering the motorcycle is a 177cc air-cooled single-cylinder engine that develops 13hp at 7,500rpm and 13Nm at 6,000rpm, coupled to a five-speed gearbox. Braking duties are handled by a 270mm front disc with a two-piston calliper and a rear drum brake. The W175 LTD also features an upright riding stance, aided by pullback handlebars, neutral-set footpegs and a flat single-piece seat.

articles.title: 'Big Short' investor Michael Burry is not impressed with Anthropic valuation; says: Claude-maker's business of developing AI models is far too expensive, too much ...
articles.description: Michael Burry, known for predicting the 2008 housing crash, is questioning the high valuations of AI firm Anthropic and SpaceX. He believes hype, not fundamentals, is driving investor interest, particularly for Anthropic's AI model business, which he deems "far too expensive" and unsustainable long-term.

articles.title: US runs out of EB-2 visas for Indians this fiscal year, pauses fresh issuances until October
articles.description: The US has exhausted its annual allocation of EB-2 immigrant visas for Indian nationals, forcing a temporary halt to new issuances until October 2026. The move highlights the growing pressure on employment-based immigration pathways and could further lengthen waiting times for thousands of highly skilled Indian professionals.

articles.title: Microsoft AI CEO: My team is 'more concerned' about Anthropic, than Google, Meta and OpenAI
articles.description: Microsoft's AI chief, Mustafa Suleyman, revealed a strategic shift, prioritizing competition with Anthropic over rivals like Google and Meta. This focus stems from Anthropic's aggressive entry into enterprise software and coding tools, posing a direct threat to Microsoft's core business. Microsoft is now developing its own advanced AI models to counter this challenge and reduce reliance on OpenAI.
